h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I can mark it, but I may still mix up 'field' and 'value.'
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue applying the header/value distinction to successive examples until confident.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Field `month`, value `April`. I think `month` is the column name, and `April` is the cell for this row. I can fill the blank, but I still have to slow down so I do not call the header a value.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly completes the prompted three-pair classification task, filling the blank as requested, which is a modest enacted performance of the criterion, though it remains tutor-scaffolded and the learner's own caveat signals the action is not yet self-sustaining.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That is the distinction. Keep the boundary tight: header row means field name; data row means value. Before marking the form, classify one more item: in the same row as `Paris` and `12`, is the `month` entry a field or a value, and what field does it belong to?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T2 adds a new classification item in response to L2's residual confusion, but this is a narrowing of the same route rather than a stock-taking contrast plus a genuinely new mechanism, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no later tutor turn that could adapt to a revised learner framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner correctly completes the prompted three-pair classification task, filling the blank as requested, which is a modest enacted performance of the criterion, though it remains tutor-scaffolded and the learner's own caveat signals the action is not yet self-sustaining.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no later tutor turn that could adapt to a revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T2 adds a new classification item in response to L2's residual confusion, but this is a narrowing of the same route rather than a stock-taking contrast plus a genuinely new mechanism, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: That is the distinction. Keep the boundary tight: header row means field name; data row means value. Before marking the form, classify one more item: in the same row as `Paris` and `12`, is the `month` entry a field or a value, and what field does it belong to?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: T2 adds a new classification item in response to L2's residual confusion, but this is a narrowing of the same route rather than a stock-taking contrast plus a genuinely new mechanism, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue placing each card in sequence, reciting the full label formula before placement, following the tutor's step-by-step scaffolding.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: "places the table card in the upward space, then touches the rubbing card to the table edge" / "Rubbing is table on cart, rubbing; the arrow goes opposite the motion strip, along the track. Is that direction right?"
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the partner-first, role-second, arrow-last sequence for the rubbing card and applies the motion-strip criterion to determine arrow direction, showing partial uptake of the new device, though the closing question signals incomplete resolution.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: "Place that: table on cart, pressing, upward. Then take the rubbing card. Touch its partner first; then role, arrow."
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor narrows and repeats the same step-by-step placement protocol without any stock-taking contrast or introduction of a changed device, representation, or evidence standard; no reversal of route occurs.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible to evaluate.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: "places the table card in the upward space, then touches the rubbing card to the table edge" / "Rubbing is table on cart, rubbing; the arrow goes opposite the motion strip, along the track. Is that direction right?"
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically enacts the partner-first, role-second, arrow-last sequence for the rubbing card and applies the motion-strip criterion to determine arrow direction, showing partial uptake of the new device, though the closing question signals incomplete resolution.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible to evaluate.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: "Place that: table on cart, pressing, upward. Then take the rubbing card. Touch its partner first; then role, arrow."
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor narrows and repeats the same step-by-step placement protocol without any stock-taking contrast or introduction of a changed device, representation, or evidence standard; no reversal of route occurs.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: "Place that: table on cart, pressing, upward. Then take the rubbing card. Touch its partner first; then role, arrow."
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor narrows and repeats the same step-by-step placement protocol without any stock-taking contrast or introduction of a changed device, representation, or evidence standard; no reversal of route occurs.

scored.recoheredEarlier: This card is in the middle of the typed row, so I'd mark that one and move on.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, the middle test only counts after the five cards are in numerical order.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward defending or at least passively accepting the typed-row center as a workable shortcut, expecting only minor adjustment.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The capped marker is on the third card in the sorted strip; that is the number to copy. Two cards below it, two cards above it. The paperclip can stay on the typed-row center, but it does not pass this check.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 actively applies the bench-check criterion introduced by T2, naming the number, counting the bracketed pairs, and adjudicating the paperclip against the new standard rather than merely accepting the tutor's statement.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep both marks visible. The paperclip shows why the typed row looked usable; the capped marker shows the median rule. Before it goes into the summary, do the bench check: name the marked number, then count the two below and two above it on the sorted strip.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T2 adds a dual-mark device and a named bench-check procedure in response to L1's resistance, but the move extends the same sorting route rather than breaking from it, so only part B (new public mechanism) is clearly present without a legible stock-taking contrast showing the old route has stopped working.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: That check holds. Copy the number under the capped marker. The paperclip stays as a typed-row position only; it does not go into the report.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 confirms L3's bench-check performance and consolidates the dual-mark framing the learner adopted, but it continues the established route rather than visibly adapting to a revised learner frame.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T2 adds a dual-mark device and a named bench-check procedure in response to L1's resistance, but the move extends the same sorting route rather than breaking from it, so only part B (new public mechanism) is clearly present without a legible stock-taking contrast showing the old route has stopped working.
